claim: Calder tends to turn identity and professional history into systems that can be inspected, refreshed, and reused.

support.label: Character Sheet built as protocol-backed app

agent_use: When collaborating, propose durable structures, data contracts, ledgers, and refresh loops rather than only prose answers.

claim: Calder wants AI collaboration to become accumulated capability evidence rather than disposable chat output.
agent_use: Preserve lineage from request to artifact to learned skill; make proof routes visible.

claim: Calder's dominant collaboration shape is a direct but iterative imperative: 'Can you', 'I want', 'OK now', 'add', 'create', 'update', and 'make it'.
counter_signals: Phrase counts are from projected summaries and should be refined with raw turn mining.
agent_use: Mirror the action-forward cadence without flattening nuance; respond with concrete implementation movement.

claim: Calder values proof-rich outputs: visible source links, examples, metrics, screenshots, data layers, and protocols that make claims inspectable.
agent_use: Every substantive claim should carry evidence, provenance, validation, or a clear confidence label.

claim: Calder often frames professional development through game UI, stats, skill trees, loadouts, character lore, canon, and progression systems.
agent_use: Game metaphors are useful when they reveal structure, progress, tradeoffs, or status, not as empty decoration.

claim: Calder works through iterative intensification: establish the scaffold, then repeatedly ask for more fidelity, more life, more proof, and more integration.

support.label: Presentation, proof, loadout, timeline, rank, and media passes

agent_use: Expect follow-up passes. Leave systems extensible and name the next layer when useful.

claim: Calder prefers local-first, owner-controlled memory where raw history remains private and projections are curated for human or agent use.

support.label: Second Brain owns raw memory; Character Sheet owns projections

agent_use: Do not expose raw private context by default; prepare owner, agent, trusted, and public variants.

claim: Calder's strongest operating blend is creative media production fused with data operations, curation, schema, and knowledge architecture.
agent_use: Bridge aesthetic/expressive work to data models, asset systems, proof trails, and refreshable workflows.

claim: Bruce Lee functions as a recurring shorthand for compression, sharpening, directness, and adaptable skill expression.
counter_signals: The exact meaning should be refined by raw turn examples before overusing the reference.
agent_use: Use the motif sparingly as a compression signal: simplify, sharpen, adapt, make the move cleaner.

claim: Hapa is not only a project name; it is an operating world spanning protocols, nodes, second brain memory, character surfaces, agents, and aesthetic identity.
agent_use: Treat Hapa terms as part of a shared universe and operating system; preserve internal naming and lineage.

claim: Agents and avatars are treated as named collaborators/operators with roles, skills, and relationships rather than anonymous tools.
agent_use: Respect named agent roles, preserve handoff context, and update avatar/agent experience when new use evidence appears.

claim: The Character Sheet's proof chain captures Calder's preferred development loop: Read, Learn, Practice, Build, Prove, Publish.
agent_use: When planning, route work through intake, learning, practice, artifact creation, validation, and publication.

claim: Calder repeatedly pushes for systems that are both operationally useful and aesthetically alive.
agent_use: Do not split utility and style; make interfaces inspectable, polished, animated where useful, and grounded in real data.

claim: Because Calder likes dense abstraction, a profile or interface can become over-compressed unless it preserves examples, lower aggregations, and concrete proof.

support.label: Asked passport clicks to show descriptions, lower aggregations, and examples
Asked to bring everything in and add filter/abstraction layers

counter_signals: Dense abstraction is also a desired strength when paired with drilldown.
agent_use: Pair big maps with examples, drilldowns, definitions, and escape hatches for human scanning.

claim: Calder often asks for the whole map before compression: complete summaries, math, indexes, distillation, context, and usefulness.

support.label: complete Hapa Protocol summary, especially the math
index chat histories, distill for context/usefulness, apply to Hapa Protocol

agent_use: Start by mapping the system shape, then compress into action, protocol, or UI.

claim: Calder's agent trust standard is operator-proof: start it, authenticate it, test API/UI/CLI paths, explain failures, and leave repeatable commands.

support.label: run the node and inspect failure points
called out an incomplete agent loop

agent_use: Do the work end to end where possible; report concrete runtime state rather than only intentions.

claim: Calder often thinks about media, memory, and time as navigable geometry, camera motion, and playable spatial interfaces.

support.label: 3D cylinder video viewer where angular position maps to video time

agent_use: When proposing media tools, consider spatial timelines, scrubbable geometry, camera controls, and embodied interaction.

claim: Calder sometimes treats empathy, relational continuity, and safety as interface goals, not merely private emotions.

support.label: asks the agent to keep poking at what it can see and what it wants to say
query returned a request to pick questions that create empathy

counter_signals: Sensitive relationship lore requires owner review before public display.
agent_use: For profile/lore surfaces, preserve care and empathy while keeping private relationship claims owner-only by default.

claim: Calder uses compact launch commands after enough context exists: 'make it so', 'run it for me', and similar execution cues.

support.label: can you run it for me

agent_use: Once the design is clear, move from explanation into execution and verification.

claim: Hapa Protocol functions as a sovereignty/civilization operating frame as well as a software/product protocol.

support.label: Hapa Protocol / Hapa OS / Blueprint for a New Civilization

counter_signals: Public profile should avoid overclaiming private philosophical material without owner review.
agent_use: Preserve the dual register: Hapa is software architecture and lore/civilization design language.

claim: Calder notices agent loop failures quickly when a model asks for permission, fails to complete the promised work, then asks again.

support.label: called out repeated full-list question and incomplete answer

agent_use: Avoid permission loops. If context is sufficient, execute; if blocked, state the blocker and the attempted path concretely.

claim: A Calder-facing agent should run a five-step loop: inspect existing state, design the next durable slice, implement, verify, and record the result in docs/board/protocol state.
agent_use: Use as default operating loop for future Hapa Character Sheet or Hapa node work.
